Patch Set 3:

thanks. I think we shouldn't waste 64 bytes. this limited format has been serving us for many years. I'm fine to help with the changes required.

Patch Set 3:

a bit late thought, but still.. I think we don't even need pre_versions on a disk.

Patch Set 3:

Alex, could you explain why pre_versions fields are not needed on disk ?

Patch Set 3:

pre_versions are needed only for replay, to verify the objects are in the expected state.
if the target crashes and the request was committed, then we don't need to replay, only to reconstruct the reply. for the latter we don't need pre_versions, we only need xid->transno mapping, result and opdata. previously we didn't maintain a separate in-core structure for reconstruction, the only data was a slot in last_rcvd. with this patch this changes and we can move pre_versions into in-core representation, IMHO.

Patch Set 2:

(9 comments)

I forgot to commit these comments on the previous version of the patch.  Sorry for the delay.

The major defect is the use of BITS_TO_LONGS() returns the number of longs and not the number of bytes.  That can lead to random memory corruption.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review-1

(3 comments)

Found one more problem with the new locking for client cleanup blocking for memory allocation and IO.

Maybe the INCOMPAT flag could only be cleared upon a remount and eviction of all clients? I don't think it is critical to enable and disable this feature at runtime since it is only a server-side flag and only should prevent downgrade of the server without removing last_rcvd, rather than blocking the connections from old clients, since old clients can still use their single last_rcvd slot.

Patch Set 4:

(2 comments)

Here is the new proposed rational about OBD_INCOMPAT_MULTI_RPCS flag.

The flag is set as soon as a client that supports MULTIRPC feature connects, and remain set even if that client disconnects.

The flag is cleared (to allow downgrade of Lustre version on the MDT server) when the MDT target is started and there is no client to recover, or recovery was aborted.

Patch Set 4:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 4:

pardon, I forgot to explain why clearing at umount is better - otherwise the customer needs to mount a filesystem with the newer code just to clear the flag. so, the sequence would be : stop all the clients, umount, mount to clear the flag, umount, mount with the old version. compared to 3 steps: stop the clients, umount, mount with the old version.

Patch Set 4:

Unfortunately clearing the flag when unmounting the target is not possible when the filesystem has several MDTs, since the cross-MDT clients will still be connected.

parents.subject: LU-5319 mdc: manage number of modify RPCs in flight

commit.subject: LU-5319 mdt: support multiple modify RCPs in parallel
commit.message:
LU-5319 mdt: support multiple modify RCPs in parallel

This patch implements the server part of the feature that allows
support of multiple modify RPCs in parallel on MDT targets.

Each target export is able to store several in-memory reply
data so that it can reconstruct several requests issued by
the client in parallel.

Additionally, a new internal file REPLY_DATA is created on
the target to store on-disk reply data. The reply data slots
in that file are managed by a bitmap (lut_reply_bitmap) and
can be used to store reply data of any client.

When target recovers, the on-disk reply data is used to
restore the in-memory reply data and ensure reconstruction
of committed operations.

Signed-off-by: Alex Zhuravlev <alexey.zhuravlev@intel.com>
Signed-off-by: Gregoire Pichon <gregoire.pichon@bull.net>
Change-Id: I8f91666f5b0b4f7b9445a01c520d73f56d059ff3
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/14860
